sections.paragraph:
not used in Kal, to rain, like the Chald., Syr., Arab.

Hiphil, to rain, to pour down rain, Gen 2:5 7:4 Amo 4:7 used of God, Isa 5:6 (here more fully הִמְטִיר מָטָר), followed by עַל upon anything, Am. Isa. loc. cit. It is applied to other things which God pours down from heaven in great abundance like rain, as hail, Exo 9:18, 23 lightnings, Psa 11:6 fire with sulphur, Gen 19:24 Eze 38:22 manna, or heavenly bread, Exo 16:4 Psa 78:24. Constr. with an acc. of the thing rained down, and עַל of that upon which it descends like rain (see locc. citt.); once with בְּ of the thing rained down, Job 20:23 (see under לְחוּם ).

Niphal, to be watered with rain, Amo 4:7. Hence-
